_meta.note: All examples captured from live API calls against run_id 1 (externalRunId).

endpoints.name: Start Run
endpoints.notes: run_id is the externalRunId from ProjectVersionAiRun. definition_changed: true forces full Stage 1-4 (competitor matching + confirmation); false skips Stage 1 and goes straight to generation using existing confirmedCompetitors; omit for auto-detection. Rate limited to 20/hour. After starting, poll GET /runs/{run_id}/status.

endpoints.name: Poll Run Status
endpoints.notes: Status progression: pending -> matching (S1) -> awaiting_confirmation -> generating (S2) -> parsing (S3) -> completing (S4) -> completed. Any stage can transition to failed. When status=awaiting_confirmation, frontend must read competitorSnapshot from DB, let user confirm, save confirmedCompetitors to DB, then call the confirm endpoint. Note: when Stage 1 is skipped (definition_changed=false), matching/awaiting_confirmation do not occur. Fast stages (parsing/completing) may not be observable when polling at 1s intervals.

endpoints.name: Get Competitor List (DB read, not an API call)
endpoints.notes: Frontend reads ProjectVersionAiRun.competitorSnapshot directly from the database when status=awaiting_confirmation. Look up the row by externalRunId. Each entry in competitors[] has yougov_brand_label (canonical name to display/confirm), nielsen fields and data-availability flags. After the user confirms a selection, the frontend must write the chosen brand names (array of strings from yougov_brand_label) to the confirmedCompetitors JSONB column on the same row BEFORE calling POST /runs/{run_id}/competitors/confirm.

endpoints.name: Confirm Competitors (approved)

response.message: Competitors confirmed, proceeding to allocation generation

endpoints.notes: Only valid while status=awaiting_confirmation. run_id in body must match URL. confirmedCompetitors must already be saved to DB by the frontend, otherwise 400. On success Stage 2-4 runs in background; continue polling status.

endpoints.name: Confirm Competitors (dismissed)
endpoints.notes: Cancels the run; status becomes 'cancelled'. The run can be started again later with POST /api/v1/runs.

endpoints.name: Search Competitor

results.warning: No Nielsen spend data available for this brand.

response.warning: Brand not found in our database.

endpoints.notes: Search order: competitorSnapshot first (source='snapshot'), then YouGov + Nielsen databases (source='database'). has_yougov_data / has_nielsen_data indicate data availability; a YouGov-only match carries warning 'No Nielsen spend data available for this brand.'. found=false with warning 'Brand not found in our database.' when nothing matches. Matching is case-insensitive, partial, and umlaut-tolerant (Muller matches Müller).

endpoints.name: Get Result

allocations.reasoning: Landliebe spent €79,305,265 on FERNSEHEN with a +5.76pp KPI change. Zott Sahne Joghurt spent €18,271,818 with a -0.32pp change. FERNSEHEN dominates competitor spend (approx. 75% of total market spend on this channel), recommending 50% allocation due to Landliebe's significant KPI uplift.
Landliebe spent €12,796,993 on RADIO with a +5.76pp KPI change. Despite not being the largest spend, it achieved a high KPI uplift, recommending 15% allocation.

Landliebe spent €6,318,249 on ONLINE with a +5.76pp KPI change, while Dany Sahne spent €2,785,618 with -1.25pp. ONLINE received significant positive results from Landliebe, suggesting a 20% allocation.
Landliebe spent €6,166,738 on PLAKAT with a +5.76pp KPI change. Zott Sahne Joghurt spent €1,529,198 with a -0.32pp change. Given Landliebe's success, a 10% allocation is recommended.

Zott Sahne Joghurt spent €2,205,865 on ZEITUNGEN with a -0.32pp KPI change, which is the smallest decline among non-positive changes. This indicates a relatively stable channel, thus a minimal 5% allocation.

response.reasoning_summary: Using Landliebe and Zott Sahne Joghurt as benchmarks (combined spend focused on FERNSEHEN and RADIO with significant KPI changes of +5.76pp and -0.32pp). Strategy focuses on channels where Landliebe achieved the highest ad awareness uplift.

endpoints.notes: Only available when status=completed (otherwise 400). Returns the allocationResult JSON verbatim. allocations[] entries use channel, share_pct, budget_gross_eur, reasoning.

endpoints.name: Get Chat History

messages.content:
Total Budget: EUR 1,000,000

Allocation:
  • TV: EUR 600,000 (60.0%)
  • Radio: EUR 150,000 (15.0%)
  • Digital: EUR 150,000 (15.0%)
  • OOH: EUR 50,000 (5.0%)
  • Print: EUR 50,000 (5.0%)

Reasoning: Using Landliebe and Zott Sahne Joghurt as benchmarks (combined spend €113,785,723, KPI changes of +5.76pp and -0.32pp). Strategy focuses on leveraging FERNSEHEN and ONLINE for high impact due to their strong performance in Landliebe's media strategy.

Total Budget: EUR 1,000,000

Allocation:
  • TV: EUR 600,000 (60.0%)
  • Radio: EUR 150,000 (15.0%)
  • Digital: EUR 150,000 (15.0%)
  • OOH: EUR 50,000 (5.0%)
  • Print: EUR 50,000 (5.0%)

Reasoning: Using Landliebe and Zott Sahne Joghurt as benchmarks (combined spend €103,240,327, KPI changes of +5.76pp and -0.32pp respectively). Strategy focuses on channels where Landliebe achieved substantial positive KPI changes, indicating high efficiency.
Please update this in the Definition Area and click Generate to rerun the allocation.

endpoints.notes: Returns messages from chatSnapshot. role is 'system', 'user' or 'agent'. System feedback cards (allocation summary, warnings) are inserted automatically at the start when a run completes; cards have card_type 'allocation_summary' or 'warning' and a string id ('summary_0', 'warning_0'), while user/agent messages have int ids and card_type null. NOTE: each run completion prepends a new summary card and older cards remain, so multiple cards with id 'summary_0' can appear after reruns - the newest card is first. limit defaults to 50, returns most recent messages; total_count is the full count.

endpoints.name: Send Chat Message (Q&A)

request_body.message: How much was allocated to TV?

response.agent_response: The budget allocated to TV is EUR 500,000, which represents 50.0% of the total budget.

endpoints.notes: Simple mode Q&A: the agent answers questions about the allocation result using LLM context (allocations, competitors, reasoning). project_id/version_id optional.

endpoints.name: Send Chat Message (edit attempt in simple mode)

response.agent_response: Please update this in the Definition Area and click Generate to rerun the allocation.

endpoints.notes: In simple mode (chat_agent_mode=false) the agent does not modify inputs; edit requests get a redirect/explanation response and no changes are made (rerun_triggered=false, updated_inputs=null).

endpoints.name: Download Debug ZIP
endpoints.notes: Binary download (Content-Type: application/zip, filename run_{run_id}_debug.zip). Only exists when the server runs with STAGE1_DEBUG_MODE=True and the run produced debug files. ZIP contains per-stage debug files from debug_output/run_{run_id}/: Stage 1 matching steps (Y1_industry_resolution, Y2_brand_and_competitors, Y3_yougov_filtered_data, N1_nielsen_produktmarke_filtering, FINAL_filtered_data), allocation generation (S2_prompt.txt, S2_llm_response.txt, S2_parsed_raw.json, S2_final_result.json), and chat agent files (C0_context_loaded, C1_intent_classification, C2_simple_mode_response). Returns 404 if debug mode disabled or run not completed.
